concepts.title: Ethers
concepts.conceptBody:
Ethers

Ethers have a general formula $\text{R}-\text{O}-{{\text{R}}^{\prime }}$. The groups $\text{R}$ and ${{\text{R}}^{\prime }}$ may be same or different and accordingly ethers are known as simple or mixed respectively. The general IUPAC name of ethers is alkoxy alkane.

concepts.title: Physical Properties of Ethers
concepts.conceptBody:
Physical Properties

  • The polar nature of the $\text{C}-\text{O}$ bond (due to the electronegativity difference of the atoms) results in intermolecular dipole-dipole interactions.
  • An ether cannot form hydrogen bonds with other ether molecules since there is no $\text{H}$ to be donated (no - OH group)
  • Ethers can be involved in H-bonding with systems able to donate $H$ (e.g. water).
  • The implications of these effects are:
    • lower melting and boiling points compared to analogous alcohols.
    • solubility in aqueous media similar to analogous alcohols.
  • Ethers have a definite dipole moment. This is due to the bent structure of their molecule.
  • They are lighter than water. Their density incolases with increase in molecular mass.

concepts.title: By Williamson Synthesis
concepts.conceptBody:
Methods of Preparation:

By Williamson Synthesis: 

$\text{R}-\text{X}+{{\text{R}}^{\prime }}\text{ONa}\xrightarrow{{}}\text{R}-\text{O}-{{\text{R}}^{\prime }}+\text{NaX}$

Here $-\text{X}$ can be halides, sulphates $\left( -\text{OS}{{\text{O}}_{2}}\text{OR} \right)$ or sulphonates $\left( -\text{OS}{{\text{O}}_{2}}\text{R} \right)$. Also, the temperature should be low for this reaction.

The alkyl halide used in this reaction should preferably be primary because in case of secondary and tertiary alkyl halides, the elimination reaction predominates resulting in the formation of alkene as the major product.

Whereas,

Note: (1) For aromatic ethers generally alkyl sulphates are used.

(2) The reaction is ${{S}_{N}}2.$

concepts.title: Dehydration of Alcohols
concepts.conceptBody:
Dehydration of alcohols

$\underset{\text{ (excess) }}{\mathop{2{{\text{C}}_{2}}{{\text{H}}_{5}}\text{OH}}}\,\frac{\text{ conc}\text{. }{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}}{413~\text{K}}{{\text{C}}_{2}}{{\text{H}}_{5}}-\text{O}-{{\text{C}}_{2}}{{\text{H}}_{5}}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}$

$2{{\text{C}}_{2}}{{\text{H}}_{5}}\text{OH}\xrightarrow{A{{l}_{2}}{{O}_{3}}}{{\text{C}}_{2}}{{\text{H}}_{5}}-\text{O}-{{\text{C}}_{2}}{{\text{H}}_{5}}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}$

Dehydration of alcohols to ether is an example of nucleophillic substitution. However, it can be used only to prepare symmetrical ethers only, otherwise we get a mixture of ethers. For example,

$\text{R}-\text{OH}+\text{HO}{{\text{R}}^{\prime }}\xrightarrow[140\,\,C,\Delta ]{conc.\,\,{{H}_{2}}S{{O}_{4}}}\text{R}-\text{OR}+{{\text{R}}^{\prime }}\text{OR}+{{\text{R}}^{\prime }}\text{O}{{\text{R}}^{\prime }}$

concepts.title: Alkoxy Mercuration - Demercuration
concepts.conceptBody:
Alkoxy Mercuration - Demercuration of Alkenes

Note: 1. Product is formed following Markownikov’s rule.

2. Reaction proceeds via the formation of a cyclic mercurinium ion

concepts.title: Preparation of Epoxides
concepts.conceptBody:
Preparation of epoxides

(a)

$\text{RC}{{\text{O}}_{3}}\text{H}$ is peroxy acid.

(b) epoxides can also be prepared by internal ${{\text{S}}_{\text{N}}}2$ reaction of chlorohydrin
